In addition, strategic reserves and ongoing dialogues with global energy organizations and the United States reinforce India's readiness against any supply disruptions.

Lasers, satellites and cyberattacks: How US is crippling Iran's arsenal
description: Although the fighting is taking place in the Middle East, much of the monitoring work is carried out thousands of kilometres away. Personnel in the United States operate from facilities housing large radar domes known as radomes. These spherical structures resemble giant golf balls and collect satellite data in real time. Analysts use the information to determine missile trajectories and likely targets.

This move places him alongside other tech titans like Larry Page and Mark Zuckerberg, who have also invested heavily in Miami's luxury real estate market, driving up prices.

description: India has formally expressed condolences for the death of Iran's Supreme Leader, with the Foreign Secretary visiting the Iranian Embassy. Amidst escalating regional tensions, Prime Minister Modi has engaged in extensive diplomatic outreach, speaking with leaders of several Gulf nations and Israel. These discussions focused on regional security and the welfare of the large Indian diaspora.

title: From Dhoni to Yuvraj: Cricket’s biggest names attend Arjun Tendulkar’s wedding
description: Indian cricket's biggest names assembled in Mumbai for Arjun Tendulkar's wedding. Stalwarts from various eras attended the grand celebration. Notable guests included MS Dhoni, Suresh Raina, Gautam Gambhir, and Jay Shah. Former coaches Ravi Shastri and Rahul Dravid were also present. Several former players and administrators marked their presence. Arjun married Saaniya Chandhok.

description: A second Iranian warship is en route to Sri Lanka after a US submarine destroyed the IRIS Dena frigate, killing 87 sailors. Sri Lanka is discussing Iran's request for the second vessel to enter its waters for safety, with rescued sailors receiving treatment in Galle.

title: 'It’s been 5 days': Sammy hits panic button amid tensions in Middle East
description: The West Indies team is stranded in India after their T20 World Cup exit due to Middle East airspace restrictions caused by Israel–Iran tensions. Flights have been disrupted, delaying their return home. Frustrated coach Daren Sammy posted, “I just wanna go home” and “At least an update… It’s been 5 days.” Cricket West Indies says it’s working with the ICC to arrange safe travel.

The PCB is reviewing his conduct, but he may remain coach because he has a two-year contract and the board wants stability.

The couple, along with Amitabh and Jaya Bachchan, added a touch of Bollywood glamour to the high-profile event, reaffirming Aishwarya's enduring grace.

Trump's gold coup: Venezuela to ship a ton of bullion to US
description: Venezuela's state-owned mining company, Minerven, has agreed to sell up to 1,000 kilograms of gold dore bars to commodities trader Trafigura, with the gold destined for US markets. This multimillion-dollar deal, involving 98% final gold content, highlights strengthening US-Venezuela commercial ties under the Trump administration's supervision.

With Amit Shah by his side, Bihar CM Nitish Kumar files nomination for Rajya Sabha
description: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar filed his Rajya Sabha nomination today, accompanied by Union Home Minister Amit Shah. This move follows his decision to step down and head to Parliament, fulfilling a long-held aspiration. The transition, occurring after a recent election victory, signals a potential shift in Bihar's leadership within the NDA.

title: New York Yankees linked to $330M Philadelphia Phillies star to pair with Aaron Judge
description: The New York Yankees are reportedly exploring a trade for Bryce Harper from the Philadelphia Phillies during the 2026 preseason. Such a move would pair Harper with Aaron Judge, significantly boosting the Yankees' offensive power. This potential deal could see the Yankees send outfield depth and prospects to Philadelphia, while the Phillies would gain financial flexibility and farm system depth.

title: Connor McDavid sends clear message as Edmonton Oilers face trade deadline
description: Connor McDavid quickly endorsed the Oilers’ trade for Connor Murphy, stressing the need to add talent without subtracting key pieces. With Edmonton firmly in a win now phase and draft picks holding less value, the move reflects urgency around the Stanley Cup chase. Chicago gains a future asset, while the Oilers strengthen their blue line for an immediate playoff push.

title: B'luru traffic tangle: Police plan right-side driving at key junctions to ease gridlock
description: Bengaluru police are piloting a shift to right-side driving on congested roads near junctions to ease traffic. This American-style system, already successful near Garuda Mall, aims to allow vehicles exiting the city to move more freely, reducing bottlenecks during peak hours.

title: Mark Zuckerberg is done with Meta's highest-paid employee, this reorganisation proves
description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has quietly dismantled Alexandr Wang's power nine months after the $14 billion hire. The 28-year-old data labeling entrepreneur now oversees AI models being built on infrastructure he doesn't control. A new applied AI engineering organization under Andrew Bosworth routes talent and data pipelines around Wang entirely. Researchers report to other executives. Wang keeps his title but loses every lever that matters—a calculated pivot that suggests Zuckerberg's bet has already failed.

title: Senior Pentagon official breaks silence on Anthropic-department of war dispute
description: A Pentagon official revealed alarming AI contract restrictions that could halt military operations.  These limitations, found in contracts with Anthropic, prevented planning for potential combat scenarios.  The dispute escalated when an AI executive questioned its use in a high-profile mission, leading to Anthropic being deemed a security risk and banned from government work, while OpenAI secured a new deal.

title: Draymond Green reacts to Blackpink K-Pop song mentioning his name
description: Golden State Warriors star Draymond Green expressed excitement after being name-dropped by the popular Korean girl group Blackpink. It was in their new song "Me and My." Green, a four-time NBA champion, views the K-Pop mention as a significant validation of his global influence and popularity. He plans to listen to the song with his daughters, who are fans of K-Pop.
